front: Treatise: co-written with Jean Metzinger as the first major theoretical treatise on Cubism
Treatise: argues that Cubism is based on non-Euclidean (Riemannian) geometry rather than Euclidean geometry
Treatise: reproduces Gleizes's painting The Bathers, which depicts bathers in front of smoke-producing factories in Clichy
Treatise: Guillaume Apollinaire's The Cubist Painters described this manifesto's co-author Gleizes as representing the 'scientific' branch of Cubism
Painting: depicts bathers in front of factories producing swirling smoke from Clichy in the background, at La Grande Jatte
Painting: exhibited at the 1912 Salon des Independants; one of five Gleizes depictions of La Grande Jatte
Painting: depicts a man with a golden hunting horn, with a group of hunting dogs to his right
Painting: one of the largest Cubist paintings, equaled only by Robert Delaunay's La Ville de Paris
Movement: Cubist collective associated with Cubism and Orphism whose meetings were held at Gleizes's home; named 'Golden Section' by Josephin Peladan for the quantity phi
Artist: Gleizes and Metzinger rejected Marcel Duchamp's Nude Descending a Staircase No. 2 from the 1912 Salon des Independants, feeling the nude should be shown reclining
Artist: forger John Myatt began his career after his mountain village painting imitating this artist was accepted by Christie's
Artist: chronicled the fortunes of the Salon d'Automne in The Epic: From Immobile Form to Mobile Form
Artist: made money from the 1913 Armory Show alongside Jacques Villon
